decision_summary:
The current design is feasible with a chip max temperature of ~43.2°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. The current value is at the upper bound (500.0), but the editable variable definition allows increasing it (RecommendedDirection=increase) and the bounds check suggests the system might accept a push or the previous step didn't fully saturate the physical potential in this specific solver context. However, strictly adhering to the provided bounds [20.0, 500.0], the value is already at the maximum. Re-reading the rules: 'Bounds=[20.0, 500.0]'. Since the current value is 500.0, I cannot increase it further without violating the explicit bounds provided in the prompt text. 

Correction: The current value is 500.0. The bounds are [20.0, 500.0]. I cannot increase conductivity. I must look at other levers. 
Next best lever: components.2.width (spreader width). Current is 0.9. Bounds [0.2, 1.0]. I can increase width to 1.0 to maximize lateral spreading area.
Alternative: components.2.height. Current 0.1. Bounds [0.05, 0.18]. Can increase to 0.15 (within 1.5x ratio). 
Strategy: Increase spreader width to the maximum allowed (1.0) to enhance lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Maximizing the heat spreader width increases the cross-sectional area for lateral heat conduction towards the Dirichlet boundaries, helping to lower the peak chip temperature further.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
risk_notes: Approaching geometric limits of the design domain
Diminishing returns as width approaches the full domain size
